medicines.name: Warfarin
medicines.warnings: Major bleeding risk. Requires regular INR blood monitoring. Do not take with NSAIDs or Aspirin.
medicines.uses: Prophylaxis and treatment of venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, and thromboembolic complications associated with atrial fibrillation.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits Vitamin K epoxide reductase, depleting functional Vitamin K-dependent clotting factors (II, VII, IX, and X).
medicines.storage: Store at 20°C to 25°C (68°F to 77°F) in a tight, light-resistant container.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic antiplatelet and anticoagulant effects, compounded by mucosal irritation.
interactions.impact: Extreme risk of severe internal gastrointestinal bleeding and hemorrhagic stroke.
interactions.action: Avoid combination. Use alternative analgesics like Paracetamol. If mandatory, monitor INR daily and prescribe proton pump inhibitors.

interactions.mechanism: NSAID-induced inhibition of platelet aggregation and gastric mucosal damage combined with anticoagulant actions.
interactions.impact: High risk of acute gastrointestinal bleeding and ulceration.
interactions.action: Contraindicated. Discontinue Ibuprofen. Substitute with Acetaminophen (Paracetamol) for pain control.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP2C9 metabolic pathway of Warfarin, increasing Warfarin plasma concentration.
interactions.impact: Elevated INR, leading to spontaneous bruising and bleeding.
interactions.action: Reduce Warfarin dose by 30-50% when initiating Amiodarone. Check INR twice weekly.

medicines.name: Aspirin
medicines.warnings: Gastrointestinal bleeding risk. Risk of Reye's syndrome in children. Avoid in patients with active ulcers.
medicines.uses: Management of mild to moderate pain, inflammation, and fever. Lower doses (81mg) are used as cardioprotective antiplatelet agents.
medicines.mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits cyclooxygenase-1 and 2 (COX-1 and COX-2) enzymes, preventing prostaglandin and thromboxane A2 synthesis.
medicines.storage: Keep in a cool, dry place. Avoid moisture.

interactions.mechanism: Increased bleeding tendency via dual pathways (antiplatelet + anticoagulant).
interactions.impact: Severe internal bleeding, mucosal hemorrhage.
interactions.action: Discontinue Aspirin unless specifically prescribed as low-dose dual antiplatelet therapy. Monitor closely.

interactions.mechanism: Ibuprofen competitively blocks Aspirin access to the COX-1 active site, diminishing cardioprotective antiplatelet effects.
interactions.impact: Decreased cardiac protective efficacy of low-dose Aspirin, increased gastric irritation.
interactions.action: Take Aspirin at least 30 minutes before or 8 hours after Ibuprofen.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.warnings: May increase the risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events, myocardial infarction, and stroke. Can cause severe gastrointestinal bleeding.
medicines.uses: Relief of signs and symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis and osteoarthritis. Relief of mild to moderate pain and treatment of primary dysmenorrhea.
medicines.mechanism: Reversibly inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, decreasing synthesis of inflammatory prostaglandins.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a tightly closed container.

interactions.mechanism: Dual blockade of clotting mechanisms and gastric barrier destruction.
interactions.impact: Dangerous gastrointestinal bleeding and hemorrhage.
interactions.action: Contraindicated. Do not prescribe together. Switch to Paracetamol.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs decrease vasodilator prostaglandins, causing renal afferent arteriolar constriction while Lisinopril dilates efferent arterioles, drastically lowering GFR.
interactions.impact: Acute renal failure and loss of blood pressure control.
interactions.action: Avoid concurrent use in elderly or kidney patients. Monitor creatinine levels and blood pressure if used together.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin
medicines.warnings: Contraindicated in patients with known penicillin hypersensitivity (allergic reaction). Can cause severe anaphylaxis.
medicines.uses: Treatment of infections of the ear, nose, throat, genitourinary tract, skin, and lower respiratory tract caused by susceptible bacteria.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis by binding to penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs), leading to bacterial cell lysis.
medicines.storage: Keep oral suspension refrigerated. Store capsules at room temperature.

interactions.mechanism: Penicillins compete for active renal tubular secretion of methotrexate, increasing its plasma levels.
interactions.impact: Methotrexate toxicity (bone marrow suppression, oral ulcers).
interactions.action: Avoid combination. Monitor methotrexate blood concentrations closely if co-administered.

medicines.name: Lisinopril
medicines.warnings: Can cause fetal harm or death when used in pregnancy. Risk of angioedema (life-threatening swelling of throat/face). Can cause dry cough.
medicines.uses: Treatment of hypertension, heart failure (as adjunctive therapy), and to improve survival after myocardial infarction.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), blocking conversion of angiotensin I to the potent vasoconstrictor angiotensin II, thereby reducing systemic vascular resistance and aldosterone secretion.
medicines.storage: Store at 15°C to 30°C (59°F to 86°F) in a well-closed container.

long_term: Kidney function preservation in diabetic nephropathy (but contraindicated in bilateral renal artery stenosis)

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit vasodilatory prostaglandins, reducing Lisinopril effectiveness and triggering acute renal constriction.
interactions.impact: Hypertensive rebound, acute kidney injury.
interactions.action: Monitor renal panels and blood pressure. Minimize NSAID usage; use Paracetamol for pain.

interactions.mechanism: Additive potassium-retaining effects of Spironolactone (aldosterone antagonist) and Lisinopril (decreases aldosterone).
interactions.impact: Severe hyperkalemia (high blood potassium) leading to cardiac arrhythmias and arrest.
interactions.action: Monitor serum potassium levels weekly. Advise low-potassium diet. Keep Spironolactone dosage low (e.g. 12.5-25mg).

medicines.name: Metformin
medicines.warnings: Lactic acidosis (rare but fatal build up of lactic acid in blood). Contraindicated in patients with severe renal impairment (eGFR < 30 mL/min).
medicines.uses: First-line medication for the treatment of type 2 diabetes, particularly in overweight people.
medicines.mechanism: Decreases hepatic glucose production, decreases intestinal absorption of glucose, and improves insulin sensitivity by increasing peripheral glucose uptake and utilization.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and direct sunlight.

interactions.mechanism: Iodinated contrast can cause acute renal failure, which results in Metformin accumulation and severe lactic acidosis.
interactions.impact: Metformin toxicity and life-threatening lactic acidosis.
interactions.action: Discontinue Metformin prior to, or at the time of, iodinated contrast imaging and withhold for 48 hours post-procedure until renal function is verified normal.

medicines.name: Nitroglycerin
medicines.warnings: Can cause severe hypotension. Contraindicated with phosphodiesterase-5 (PDE-5) inhibitors like Sildenafil (Viagra).
medicines.uses: Acute relief of an attack or prophylaxis of angina pectoris due to coronary artery disease.
medicines.mechanism: Relaxes vascular smooth muscle. Direct dilator of coronary arteries, promoting blood redistribution and reducing venous return (preload) which lowers cardiac workload.
medicines.storage: Keep in the original glass container, tightly capped. Do not store in plastic.

long_term: Nitrate tolerance (decreased effectiveness over time, requiring a 10-12 hour nitrate-free interval daily)

interactions.mechanism: PDE-5 inhibitors prolong Nitroglycerin-induced cGMP-mediated vasodilation, leading to systemic blood pressure collapse.
interactions.impact: Severe, refractory hypotension, myocardial infarction, and death.
interactions.action: Absolutely contraindicated. Do not administer Nitroglycerin if Sildenafil was taken within 24 hours (or 48 hours for Tadalafil).

medicines.name: Sildenafil
medicines.warnings: Do not take with Nitroglycerin or other nitrates. May cause sudden vision or hearing loss. Priapism (painful erection >4 hours).
medicines.uses: Treatment of erectile dysfunction and pulmonary arterial hypertension.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits PDE-5, preventing degradation of cGMP in corpus cavernosum and pulmonary vasculature, leading to smooth muscle relaxation and increased blood flow.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic nitric oxide/cGMP pathway vasodilation causing severe hypoperfusion.
interactions.impact: Extreme blood pressure drop, coronary artery hypoperfusion, cardiac arrest.
interactions.action: Never take together. If chest pain occurs after taking Sildenafil, advise medical emergency crew immediately and do not give sublingual nitrates.

medicines.name: Albuterol
medicines.warnings: Paradoxical bronchospasm. Can cause hypokalemia and palpitations. Use with caution in cardiovascular disorders.
medicines.uses: Prevention and treatment of bronchospasm in patients with reversible obstructive airway disease (Asthma, COPD).
medicines.mechanism: Selectively binds to Beta2-adrenergic receptors in bronchial smooth muscle, activating adenyl cyclase and increasing intracellular cAMP, causing bronchodilation.
medicines.storage: Store inhaler at room temperature. Do not puncture or expose to heat.

interactions.mechanism: Beta-blockers (like Propranolol) directly antagonize Beta2-stimulant bronchodilation, while Albuterol reduces beta-blocker efficacy.
interactions.impact: Severe bronchospasm in asthmatics, loss of blood pressure/heart rate control.
interactions.action: Avoid non-selective beta-blockers in asthma patients. If a beta-blocker is mandatory, use cardioslective ones (e.g., Metoprolol) with extreme caution.

medicines.name: Propranolol
medicines.warnings: Do not discontinue abruptly (can trigger severe thyroid storm, arrhythmia, or MI). Contraindicated in asthma and severe bronchospastic diseases.
medicines.uses: Management of hypertension, angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmias, hypertrophic subaortic stenosis, pheochromocytoma, and migraine prophylaxis.
medicines.mechanism: Non-selective beta-adrenergic receptor blocker (blocks both Beta1 and Beta2 receptors), reducing heart rate, cardiac output, and blood pressure, and causing bronchiolar constriction.
medicines.storage: Store in a cool, dark place.

interactions.mechanism: Direct pharmacodynamic antagonism at Beta2-adrenergic receptors.
interactions.impact: Acute bronchospasm and rescue inhaler failure.
interactions.action: Avoid combination. Use cardioselective beta blockers (Metoprolol) or different antihypertensive classes.

medicines.name: Eliquis
medicines.warnings: Bleeding risk. Do not stop abruptly without consulting doctor. Avoid taking with NSAIDs or Aspirin.
medicines.uses: Reduction of risk of stroke and systemic embolism in non-valvular atrial fibrillation.
medicines.mechanism: Selective, reversible active-site inhibitor of Factor Xa, inhibiting free and clot-bound factor Xa.

long_term: No major cumulative long-term toxicity. Minor hepatic impact.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic antiplatelet and anticoagulant effects.
interactions.impact: Increased risk of serious bleeding.
interactions.action: Use with caution. Avoid daily Aspirin unless clinically indicated.

interactions.mechanism: NSAID-induced platelet inhibition added to factor Xa inhibition.
interactions.impact: Severe gastrointestinal bleeding risk.
interactions.action: Avoid co-prescription. Use Acetaminophen for pain relief.

medicines.name: Xarelto
medicines.warnings: Bleeding risk. Avoid with other anticoagulants, Aspirin, or NSAIDs.
medicines.uses: Treatment and prevention of deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism.
medicines.mechanism: Reversible, direct inhibitor of Factor Xa, blocking the clotting cascade.

long_term: Risk of chronic bleeding events in predisposed patients.

interactions.mechanism: Antiplatelet and factor Xa inhibition overlap.
interactions.impact: Elevated gastrointestinal bleeding risk.
interactions.action: Avoid NSAIDs/Aspirin.

interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic synergy of NSAID mucosal irritation and factor Xa blockade.
interactions.impact: Extreme risk of stomach ulcer bleeding.
interactions.action: Contraindicated. Switch to Acetaminophen.

medicines.name: Tylenol
medicines.warnings: Severe hepatotoxicity if max dose (4g/day) exceeded. Avoid alcohol while taking. Check other medications for acetaminophen content.
medicines.uses: Temporary relief of minor aches and pains and reduction of fever.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits prostaglandin synthesis in the central nervous system; weak peripheral COX inhibition.

long_term: Chronic daily use can cause chronic liver load or analgesic-induced nephropathy.

interactions.mechanism: Chronic high doses of Acetaminophen may inhibit Warfarin metabolism.
interactions.impact: Slightly elevated INR and bleeding risk if taken daily over 7 days.
interactions.action: Limit usage. Monitor INR regularly if daily dosage exceeds 2g.

medicines.name: Celebrex
medicines.warnings: Increased risk of serious cardiovascular events. Gastrointestinal bleeding risk (lower than non-selective NSAIDs).
medicines.uses: Relief of symptoms of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.
medicines.mechanism: Selective inhibitor of cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) enzyme, sparing COX-1 (gastric mucosal protector).
medicines.storage: Store in a dry place at room temperature.

long_term: Renal papillary necrosis with chronic use, elevated cardiovascular risk.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic bleeding risk and CYP2C9 metabolic overlap.
interactions.impact: Elevated INR and bleeding risk.
interactions.action: Monitor INR daily.

medicines.name: Mobic
medicines.warnings: Cardiovascular thrombotic risk. Gastrointestinal irritation and bleeding.
medicines.uses: Relief of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis pain.
medicines.mechanism: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug that inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes.

long_term: Kidney damage, gastric erosion, blood pressure elevation.

interactions.mechanism: Additive antiplatelet effect and gastric irritation.
interactions.impact: Gastrointestinal bleeding.
interactions.action: Do not combine. Switch to Tylenol.

medicines.name: Zithromax
medicines.warnings: Can cause QT prolongation and cardiac arrhythmia. Risk of hepatotoxicity.
medicines.uses: Treatment of mild to moderate bacterial infections like bronchitis, pneumonia, and skin infections.
medicines.mechanism: Binds to the 50S ribosomal subunit of susceptible microorganisms, interfering with microbial protein synthesis.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a tight container.

long_term: Gut flora disruption. High resistance risk if overused.

medicines.name: Keflex
medicines.warnings: Contraindicated in patients with cephalosporin allergy. Use with caution in penicillin-allergic patients (10% cross-reactivity).
medicines.uses: Treatment of respiratory tract infections, otitis media, skin and urinary tract infections.
medicines.mechanism: First-generation cephalosporin that inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis.

long_term: Renal loading if used long-term at high doses. Gut microflora depletion.

medicines.name: Cozaar
medicines.warnings: Fetal toxicity during pregnancy. Risk of hyperkalemia. Renal function impairment in vulnerable patients.
medicines.uses: Treatment of hypertension and protection of kidneys in diabetic patients.
medicines.mechanism: Blocks the vasoconstrictor and aldosterone-secreting effects of angiotensin II by selectively blocking AT1 receptors.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a tight container.

long_term: Excellent long-term safety profile. Kidney protective in diabetes.

interactions.mechanism: NSAID decreases renal blood flow, reducing ARB efficacy.
interactions.impact: Reduced blood pressure control and risk of renal failure.
interactions.action: Avoid NSAIDs, monitor renal parameters.

medicines.name: Norvasc
medicines.warnings: May cause peripheral edema (swelling of ankles). Risk of hypotension and worsening angina at initiation.
medicines.uses: Treatment of hypertension and chronic stable angina.
medicines.mechanism: Dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker that inhibits calcium influx into vascular smooth muscle, causing vasodilation.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature away from light.

long_term: Chronic mild edema, well tolerated over years.

medicines.name: Jardiance
medicines.warnings: Risk of necrotizing fasciitis of the perineum (Fournier's gangrene). Risk of ketoacidosis and urinary tract infections.
medicines.uses: Glycemic control in type 2 diabetes and reduction of cardiovascular death risk.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits sodium-glucose co-transporter 2 (SGLT2) in kidneys, reducing renal glucose reabsorption and increasing urinary glucose excretion.

long_term: Cardioprotective and kidney protective properties in diabetes.

medicines.name: Januvia
medicines.warnings: Risk of acute pancreatitis. Severe and disabling joint pain. Worsening heart failure.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) enzyme, increasing active incretin hormone levels to stimulate insulin release.

long_term: Generally safe, no chronic organ accumulation.

medicines.name: Imdur
medicines.warnings: Can cause severe hypotension and headache. Do not use with PDE-5 inhibitors like Sildenafil or Cialis.
medicines.uses: Prevention of angina pectoris due to coronary artery disease.
medicines.mechanism: Relaxes vascular smooth muscle, producing dilatory effects on peripheral arteries and veins.

long_term: Development of nitrate tolerance (mitigated by nitrate-free interval).

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic cGMP-mediated vasodilation.
interactions.impact: Severe hypotension collapse.
interactions.action: Never combine.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic cGMP-mediated vasodilation.
interactions.impact: Severe hypotension collapse.
interactions.action: Never combine.

medicines.name: Cialis
medicines.warnings: Do not take with Nitroglycerin or other nitrates. Worsens hypotension. Priapism risk. Sudden hearing/vision loss.
medicines.uses: Treatment of erectile dysfunction and benign prostatic hyperplasia.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits PDE-5, preventing degradation of cGMP, causing smooth muscle relaxation and blood flow.

long_term: Well tolerated. Tadalafil has a longer half-life (36 hours) than Sildenafil.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic vasodilation.
interactions.impact: Life-threatening hypotension.
interactions.action: Absolutely contraindicated.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic vasodilation.
interactions.impact: Life-threatening hypotension.
interactions.action: Absolutely contraindicated.

medicines.name: Xopenex
medicines.warnings: Paradoxical bronchospasm. Cardiovascular stimulation. Risk of hypokalemia.
medicines.uses: Treatment or prevention of bronchospasm in adults and children with reversible obstructive airway disease.
medicines.mechanism: R-enantiomer of albuterol, acting as a selective beta2-adrenergic receptor agonist causing bronchodilation.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature away from heat.

interactions.mechanism: Beta blockers directly oppose beta2 bronchodilators.
interactions.impact: Bronchospasm risk.
interactions.action: Avoid combination.

medicines.name: Spiriva
medicines.warnings: Not for acute bronchospasm. Can cause dry mouth, glaucoma worsening, or urinary retention.
medicines.uses: Maintenance treatment of bronchospasm associated with COPD and asthma.
medicines.mechanism: Long-acting, muscarinic receptor antagonist (anticholinergic) that inhibits M3 receptors in airway smooth muscle.
medicines.storage: Store capsules in original blister card at room temperature.

long_term: Generally safe. Risk of mild systemic anticholinergic effects.

medicines.name: Lopressor
medicines.warnings: Do not stop abruptly. Cardioselective but can still trigger bronchospasm in high doses. Monitor heart rate.
medicines.uses: Treatment of hypertension, angina, and hemodynamically stable heart failure.
medicines.mechanism: Beta1-selective (cardioselective) adrenoceptor blocking agent, reducing cardiac output and heart rate.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature protected from moisture.

interactions.mechanism: Cardioselective block minimizes but does not eliminate antagonism of beta2 bronchodilation.
interactions.impact: Reduced bronchodilator efficacy.
interactions.action: Use with caution. Monitor respiratory status.

medicines.name: Lipitor
medicines.warnings: Risk of rhabdomyolysis (muscle breakdown). Worsens liver enzymes. Avoid grapefruit juice.
medicines.uses: Reduction of elevated total cholesterol, LDL-C, and triglycerides.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase, the rate-limiting enzyme in cholesterol synthesis.

long_term: Elevated risk of new-onset diabetes, chronic liver enzyme elevation.

medicines.name: Synthroid
medicines.warnings: Do not use for weight loss. Risk of cardiac arrhythmias at high doses. Take on empty stomach.
medicines.uses: Replacement therapy in primary, secondary, and tertiary hypothyroidism.
medicines.mechanism: Synthetically replaces endogenous T4 thyroid hormone, which is converted to active T3 in tissue.
medicines.storage: Store protected from light and moisture.

long_term: Bone mineral density loss (osteoporosis risk) with over-replacement.

medicines.name: Nexium
medicines.warnings: Risk of bone fractures, hypomagnesemia, and C. difficile diarrhea with long term use.
medicines.uses: Treatment of GERD, healing of erosive esophagitis, and H. pylori eradication.
medicines.mechanism: Suppresses gastric acid secretion by specific inhibition of the H+/K+-ATPase proton pump.
medicines.storage: Store in a tight container at room temperature.

long_term: Osteoporosis, vitamin B12 malabsorption, chronic kidney load.

medicines.name: Prilosec
medicines.warnings: Long term use increases risk of bone fractures and kidney damage.
medicines.uses: Short-term treatment of active duodenal ulcer, GERD, and erosive esophagitis.
medicines.mechanism: Irreversibly binds to proton pump enzymes in gastric parietal cells, blocking gastric acid secretion.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a dry place.

long_term: B12 deficiency, bone density loss, dementia risk (disputed).

medicines.name: Singulair
medicines.warnings: Black box warning for serious neuropsychiatric events (aggression, depression, suicidal thoughts).
medicines.uses: Prophylaxis and chronic treatment of asthma; relief of allergic rhinitis symptoms.
medicines.mechanism: Selectively binds to cysteinyl leukotriene CysLT1 receptors, blocking airway inflammation and constriction.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture.

long_term: Generally safe, no chronic organ burden.

medicines.name: Xanax
medicines.warnings: High risk of abuse, dependence, and addiction. May cause severe central nervous system depression. Avoid alcohol.
medicines.uses: Management of anxiety disorders and short-term relief of panic disorder.
medicines.mechanism: Enhances the inhibitory neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) via direct receptor binding.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a secure location.

long_term: Cognitive decline with decades of use, high psychological tolerance.

medicines.name: Zoloft
medicines.warnings: Suicidal thoughts warning in children and young adults. Risk of serotonin syndrome. Do not stop abruptly.
medicines.uses: Treatment of major depressive disorder, panic disorder, OCD, PTSD, and social anxiety.
medicines.mechanism: Selectively inhibits presynaptic serotonin reuptake, boosting extracellular serotonin levels.

long_term: Weight gain, emotional blunting, mild hepatic metabolism adaptation.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic serotonergic activity (SSRI + Serotonin reuptake inhibitor).
interactions.impact: Serotonin syndrome (shivering, hyperthermia, seizures, death).
interactions.action: Avoid combination. Monitor closely if mandatory. Educate patient on serotonin warning signs.

medicines.name: Prozac
medicines.warnings: Suicide risk. Long half-life (requires 5-week washout before MAOIs). Serotonin syndrome risk.
medicines.uses: Acute and maintenance treatment of major depressive disorder and OCD.
medicines.mechanism: Highly selective inhibitor of neuronal serotonin reuptake in the brain.
medicines.storage: Store in a cool, dark place.

long_term: Maintains stable brain chemistry changes over years. Mild liver workload.

interactions.mechanism: CYP2D6 inhibition by Prozac prevents conversion of Tramadol to its active analgesic metabolite.
interactions.impact: Loss of pain control and elevated serotonin syndrome risk.
interactions.action: Avoid combination. Switch to different analgesic or non-SSRI antidepressant.

medicines.name: Lexapro
medicines.warnings: Suicidal ideation. High risk of QT interval prolongation in susceptible patients.
medicines.uses: Acute and maintenance treatment of major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety.
medicines.mechanism: Pure S-enantiomer of citalopram with highly specific serotonin reuptake inhibition.

long_term: Highly tolerated long-term. Very clean metabolic profile.

medicines.name: Lasix
medicines.warnings: Can lead to profound diuresis with water and electrolyte depletion. Ototoxicity at high doses.
medicines.uses: Treatment of edema associated with congestive heart failure, cirrhosis, and renal disease.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits sodium and chloride absorption in the ascending limb of the loop of Henle.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature, protect from light.

long_term: Chronic electrolyte instability, progressive kidney stress if over-prescribed.

interactions.mechanism: Additive hypotensive effects and transient renal impairment.
interactions.impact: Dizziness, severe hypotension, and elevated creatinine.
interactions.action: Use lower diuretic doses when starting ACE inhibitor.

medicines.name: Microzide
medicines.warnings: Risk of hypokalemia, hyponatremia, and hypercalcemia. Worsens gout.
medicines.uses: Adjunctive therapy in edema; management of mild to moderate hypertension.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits sodium reabsorption in the distal convoluted tubules, increasing water excretion.

long_term: Increased uric acid (gout triggers), mild glucose intolerance.

medicines.name: Aldactone
medicines.warnings: Risk of hyperkalemia. Gynecomastia in male patients. Avoid potassium supplements.
medicines.uses: Management of primary hyperaldosteronism, heart failure, and cirrhosis ascites.
medicines.mechanism: Competitively inhibits aldosterone receptors in distal renal tubules, retaining potassium.

long_term: Endocrine shifts (anti-androgenic activity). Kidney protective in heart failure.

interactions.mechanism: Dual inhibition of aldosterone pathway retains excessive potassium.
interactions.impact: Critical hyperkalemia.
interactions.action: Monitor potassium weekly. Keep doses low.

medicines.name: Plavix
medicines.warnings: Bleeding risk. Reduced efficacy in CYP2C19 poor metabolizers. Avoid taking with Omeprazole.
medicines.uses: Reduction of atherothrombotic events in patients with recent MI, stroke, or PAD.
medicines.mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits ADP binding to platelet receptors, blocking subsequent activation.

long_term: Chronic vascular fragility and bleeding risk.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic antiplatelet blockade (often used as DAPT).
interactions.impact: Enhanced cardioprotection but doubled bleeding risk.
interactions.action: Frequently co-prescribed. Monitor for bruising or black stools.

interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, preventing conversion of Clopidogrel to active form.
interactions.impact: Reduced antiplatelet efficacy, increasing stroke risk.
interactions.action: Avoid combination. Swap Omeprazole for Pantoprazole or Famotidine.

medicines.name: Bactrim
medicines.warnings: Risk of Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis. Do not use in patients with sulfa allergy.
medicines.uses: Treatment of urinary tract infections, acute otitis media, and shigellosis.
medicines.mechanism: Dual enzyme blockade of bacterial folic acid synthesis pathways.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a dry place.

long_term: Gut flora disruption. Renal toxicity if dehydrated.

medicines.name: Cipro
medicines.warnings: Black box warning for tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, and CNS effects. Risk of aortic aneurysm.
medicines.uses: Treatment of urinary tract infections, prostatitis, and lower respiratory tract infections.
medicines.mechanism: Inhibits bacterial DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV, blocking replication.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature. Avoid freezing.

long_term: Risk of persistent neuromuscular side effects (fluoroquinolone toxicity).

medicines.name: Zofran
medicines.warnings: Can cause dose-dependent QT interval prolongation. Worsens constipation.
medicines.uses: Prevention of nausea and vomiting associated with cancer chemotherapy and surgery.
medicines.mechanism: Selective 5-HT3 serotonin receptor antagonist in central and peripheral nerves.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in dark blister cards.

long_term: Mild chronic constipation. Very safe profile.

interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic effects and QT interval prolongation risks.
interactions.impact: Potential serotonin toxicity or arrhythmia.
interactions.action: Monitor closely. Check ECG if high doses are taken.

medicines.name: Claritin
medicines.warnings: Generally non-drowsy. Exercise caution in severe liver impairment.
medicines.uses: Relief of nasal and non-nasal symptoms of seasonal allergic rhinitis.
medicines.mechanism: Selective, long-acting peripheral H1-receptor antihistamine.

medicines.name: Zyrtec
medicines.warnings: May cause mild somnolence (drowsiness) in sensitive individuals. Avoid alcohol.
medicines.uses: Relief of symptoms associated with seasonal and perennial allergic rhinitis.
medicines.mechanism: Highly selective peripheral H1-receptor antagonist with minimal blood-brain barrier crossing.

medicines.name: Allegra
medicines.warnings: Do not take with fruit juices (grapefruit, apple, orange) as they reduce absorption.
medicines.uses: Relief of symptoms of seasonal allergic rhinitis and chronic idiopathic urticaria.
medicines.mechanism: Highly selective, non-sedating peripheral H1-receptor antihistamine.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature in a dry container.

medicines.name: Flonase
medicines.warnings: May cause nasal septal perforation or epistaxis (nosebleeds). Risk of localized Candida infection.
medicines.uses: Management of nasal symptoms of seasonal and perennial allergic rhinitis.
medicines.mechanism: Potent anti-inflammatory corticosteroid acting locally on nasal mucosal receptors.
medicines.storage: Store at room temperature. Do not freeze.

long_term: Localized mucosal thinning over decades. Insignificant systemic absorption.

medicines.name: Deltasone
medicines.warnings: May cause hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis suppression. Worsens infections. Can cause mood changes and fluid retention.
medicines.uses: Treatment of severe inflammatory, allergic, and autoimmune diseases.
medicines.mechanism: Systemic glucocorticoid that binds to intracellular receptors, down-regulating inflammatory genes.

long_term: Osteoporosis, Cushingoid features (moon face), cataracts, immune suppression.

interactions.mechanism: Synergistic gastric mucosal damage pathways.

analysis.recommendations: Prescription displays moderate contraindication risk. Metformin should be monitored or swapped for renal-safe alternatives like Empagliflozin.
